display.text:
model: gemma-4-26b
task: generate_mujoco_xml

instructions: |
  Generate a valid MuJoCo XML model of a simple ideal pendulum using SI units.

  Coordinate system:
    +x = right
    +y = upward
    +z = out of page

  Pendulum definition:
    - Massless rod
    - Spherical bob
    - Single hinge joint

  Joint specifications:
    - Type: hinge
    - Location: [0, 1, 0]
    - Axis: [0, 0, 1]
    - Damping: 0
    - Friction: 0
    - No losses

  Rod:
    - Length: 0.2485 m
    - Mass: 0
    - Collision: disabled

  Bob:
    - Shape: sphere
    - Diameter: 0.1 m
    - Mass: 1.0 kg

  Environment:
    - Gravity: [0, -9.81, 0]

  Initial conditions:
    - Pendulum starts horizontal
    - Bob positioned along +x from hinge
    - Initial angle: +90 degrees from vertical
    - Initial angular velocity: 0 rad/s

  MuJoCo requirements:
    - Enable energy computation
    - Exactly one hinge joint
    - Joint rotates only about z-axis
    - Use SI units

  Acceptance criteria:
    - Exactly one hinge joint
    - Rod length = 0.2485 m
    - Rod mass = 0 kg
    - Bob mass = 1 kg
    - Gravity = [0, -9.81, 0]
    - Initial angle = +90 degrees
    - Initial angular velocity = 0
    - Energy computation enabled

output_format:
  type: xml
  constraints:
    - Return only valid MuJoCo XML
    - No explanations
    - No additional text

state.title: YAML_creation/Temperature_Testing/V3_Temp_0.4/test12/ChatGPT-5.3-Instant_Pend.YAML

input.content:
<mujoco>
  <model>
    <compiler angle="degree" coordinate="local" inertiafromgeom="true"/>
    <option gravity="0 0 -9.81" integrator="RK4"/>

    <asset>
      <!-- Assets can be defined here if needed -->
    </asset>

    <worldbody>
      <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 3" dir="0 0 -1"/>
      
      <body name="pendulum_base" pos="0 1 0">
        <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
        <body name="rod" pos="0 0 0">
          <!-- Massless rod represented by a geom with 0 mass is tricky in MuJoCo, 
               usually we just define the bob at the end of the rod length. 
               Since the rod is massless and has no collision, we can just position the bob. -->
          <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
            <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0" rgba="0 0 1 1"/>
          </body>
        </body>
      </body>
    </worldbody>

    <equality>
      <!-- Energy computation can be monitored via sensor or just by the physics engine -->
    </equality>
    
    <sensor>
      <!-- Example sensor for energy if needed, but requirements say 'Enable energy computation' -->
    </sensor>
  </model>
</mujoco>

<mujoco>
  <compiler angle="degree" coordinate="local" inertiafromgeom="true"/>
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" integrator="RK4"/>

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 3" dir="0 0 -1"/>
    
    <body name="hinge_body" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge_joint" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
        <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0" rgba="0 0 1 1"/>
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>
</mujoco>

  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" integrator="RK4"/>

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 3" dir="0 0 -1"/>
    
    <body name="hinge_body" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge_joint" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
        <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0" rgba="0 0 1 1"/>
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>
</mujoco>

<mujoco>
  <compiler angle="degree" coordinate="local" inertiafromgeom="true"/>
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" integrator="RK4"/>

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 3" dir="0 0 -1"/>
    
    <body name="hinge_body" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge_joint" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <!-- The bob is at 0.2485m from the hinge along +x. 
           Since the pendulum starts horizontal, the initial angle is 90 degrees from vertical.
           In MuJoCo, hinge joint angle 0 is typically the reference position.
           If we want the pendulum to start horizontal, and gravity is -y, 
           the vertical position would be 0 degrees.
           However, the requirement says: "Initial angle: +90 degrees from vertical".
           If vertical is 0, then 90 degrees is horizontal.
           The joint is at [0, 1, 0]. The bob is at [0.2485, 1, 0].
           We can use <keyframe> to set the initial state. -->
      <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
        <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0" rgba="0 0 1 1"/>
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>

  <keyframe>
    <key name="start_horizontal" qpos="1.5708" qvel="0"/>
  </keyframe>
</mujoco>
